prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is presented in black and white and unfolds in a few distinct visual sections.

At the beginning, an outdoor scene shows a man in a uniform-like outfit and cap walking through a grassy lane bordered by dense trees or orchard rows. A dog is in the foreground, moving ahead of him toward the camera. The man follows behind at a distance, centered in the path.

The next sampled scene cuts to another outdoor view. A man is seen from behind, wearing a light cap, a short-sleeved patterned shirt, and trousers. He is positioned near a pale dirt or gravel road on the right, with dense vegetation or orchard trees on the left. He appears to be walking or standing facing down the road; his face is not visible.

The final part of the commercial changes to a plain title card. Large centered text reads “MONY,” with “MUTUAL OF NEW YORK” underneath. Below that, the card reads:

“…FOR  
‘INSURED INCOME  
PLANNING SERVICE’”

At the bottom in smaller text, it identifies “THE MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, N.Y.” The last sampled frame remains on essentially the same title card, with no visible change other than minor film/grain variation.
